title: ocotea subrutilans
intro: Ocotea subrutilans, also known as Cananga or Wild Cananga, is a tropical plant species native to South America and the Pacific Islands, belonging to the laurel family (Lauraceae). The plant's essential oil, extracted from its leaves, is rich in linalool and other compounds, which have been found to possess medicinal properties, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Ocotea subrutilans include reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and relieving symptoms of menopause and PMS. Ocotea subrutilans can be consumed as a tea, added to bath water, or used in aromatherapy through essential oil diffusers and topical applications.
